name: Xerta, Via Verde del Baix Ebre – circular route
name_original: Xerta, Vía Verde del Baix Ebre – ruta circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Xerta, located in the Baix Ebre comarca of Tarragona, Spain, is characterized by its diverse landscapes shaped by the Ebro River and surrounding terrain. The region features fertile agricultural areas, notably orange groves, and is bordered by the eastern foothills of the Iberian System, including parts of Els Ports Natural Park. A significant draw for cyclists is the extensive network of Vía Verde (Greenway) routes, converted from old railway lines, which traverse tunnels and viaducts. These routes offer varied terrain, from paved sections to gravel paths, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Xerta The most popular touring cycling route is Xerta, Via Verde del Baix Ebre – circular route, a 17.5 miles (28.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route follows the Ebro River and passes through fertile agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Old railway viaduct – Via Verde de la Terra Alta loop from Aldover, a difficult 44.6 miles (71.8 km) path. This route winds through tunnels and viaducts amidst wooded and rural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Ebro River – Xerta loop from Tivenys, a 32.5 miles (52.2 km) trail leading through the Ebro River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Touring cycling around Xerta is defined by the Ebro River, the Vía Verde network, and the foothills of Els Ports Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Xerta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Xerta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Xerta?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes around Xerta, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 2,600 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Xerta?
A: The terrain around Xerta is diverse, influenced by the Ebro River and the foothills of Els Ports Natural Park. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including paved roads, dedicated cycleways, and gravel paths, especially along the Vía Verde routes. The region is known for its fertile agricultural areas, such as orange groves, and scenic landscapes with tunnels and viaducts.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Xerta?
A: Yes, Xerta offers a good selection of routes suitable for beginners. There are 10 easy touring cycling routes available. These routes often follow the Ebro River or sections of the Vía Verde, providing a gentler cycling experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Xerta?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975287527/xerta-via-verde-del-baix-ebre-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xerta, Via Verde del Baix Ebre – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 17.5-mile (28.2 km) ride following the Ebro River through agricultural areas.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling in Xerta?
A: While cycling, you can experience the Ebro River, which is a central feature of the landscape. The Vía Verde routes themselves are a major attraction, passing through old railway tunnels and over viaducts. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1074665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mont Caro from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt; or other mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass&lt;/a&gt; in the nearby Els Ports Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19826913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway viaduct – Via Verde de la Terra Alta loop from Aldover&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a notable viaduct.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Xerta?
A: The Vía Verde routes are generally well-suited for families due to their relatively flat gradients and car-free environment. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the presence of 10 easy routes suggests options for less experienced riders, including families. Local services also offer bicycle rentals, including electric bikes, which can make cycling more accessible for all family members.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Xerta?
A: The region around Xerta is pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful, with the blossom of orange trees perfuming the air along the Vía Verde. The natural thermal basin of Fontcalda, with its 22-degree springs, suggests comfortable conditions even outside peak summer. However, it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before your trip.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Xerta?
A: The touring cycling routes in Xerta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the scenic beauty of the Ebro River valley, the unique experience of the Vía Verde, and the varied landscapes that cater to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there services like bike rentals or shuttle services for cyclists in Xerta?
A: Yes, Xerta is well-equipped for touring cyclists. Companies in the area specialize in both electric and conventional bicycle rentals. Additionally, shuttle services are available, particularly for the Vía Verde de la Val de Zafán, allowing cyclists to be transported to starting points for a comfortable, often downhill, ride back to Xerta.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or rest stops along the cycling routes?
A: Along the Vía Verde, you can find convenient stops such as the Bot Station Bar, an old railway station in Bot that has been converted into a bar. The towns and villages along the Ebro River and Vía Verde routes will also offer various cafes and places to rest and refuel.

Q: Can I combine cycling with other activities in Xerta?
A: Absolutely. The Ebro River offers opportunities for activities like kayaking. The nearby Els Ports Natural Park provides options for extended mountain tours and active tourism. You can also explore historical landmarks in Xerta itself, such as Casa Pau and Casa Ceremines, or visit the Assut of Xerta (weir), a historical monument.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Many routes in Xerta feature views of the Ebro River. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro River – Xerta loop from Tivenys&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.5-mile (52.2 km) trail specifically designed to showcase the river valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro River – Xerta loop from Xerta&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.4-mile (34.4 km) route that also provides excellent river vistas.
tips: The parish church of Xerta (Baix Ebre). Next to the door there is a brick reproduction that represents a staff gauge, where the water levels that the Ebro river has reached in the various floods that Xerta has suffered since 1617 are reproduced. During the civil war, the building was saved from the widespread burning of churches, but not their old side altars and the outstanding baroque altarpiece of the main altar, which were uprooted and burned in the Plaza Mayor.
Xerta is a perfect town to make a stop to visit and to regain strength in one of the bars in the main square in the center.

Old train station in the municipality of Benifallet converted into tourist accommodation and a restaurant. The station is located in the middle of the Zafán Valley Greenway that joins Bajo Aragón and Baix Ebre.
At a lofty height, an old railway viaduct leads right through the gorge - and on the other side the Vía Verde disappears into a tunnel again.
